comprehension.subject: The Moon
comprehension.text: The Moon is Earth's closest neighbour in space. It is a big ball of rock that travels around the Earth. The Moon does not make its own light — it reflects light from the Sun, which is why it glows at night. The Moon is much smaller than the Earth — you could fit about 50 Moons inside the Earth! As the Moon travels around us, we see different shapes of it — these are called phases. Sometimes we see a full circle called a full moon. Sometimes we see just a thin curved shape called a crescent. There is no air or water on the Moon, so nothing can live there. In 1969, astronaut Neil Armstrong became the first human to walk on the Moon. He said it felt like walking on soft sand!

questions.question: Where does the Moon get its light from?
options: It reflects light from the Sun
questions.correct: It reflects light from the Sun

questions.question: What are the different shapes of the Moon called?
Who was the first human to walk on the Moon?
What is a crescent?
options: A thin curved shape of the Moon
A Moon phase seen only in winter
The dark side of the Moon
questions.correct: A thin curved shape of the Moon

comprehension.subject: Dinosaurs
comprehension.text: Dinosaurs lived on Earth millions of years ago. Some dinosaurs were as small as chickens, while others were taller than buildings! Plant-eating dinosaurs used long necks to reach leaves high in trees. Meat-eating dinosaurs had sharp teeth and strong legs for hunting. Scientists who study dinosaurs are called paleontologists. They learn about dinosaurs by digging up fossils, which are the preserved bones or footprints of ancient animals. No people ever saw real dinosaurs because they disappeared long before humans existed.

questions.question: What do scientists use to learn about dinosaurs?
What is a scientist who studies dinosaurs called?
What did meat-eating dinosaurs use for hunting?
Did humans live with dinosaurs?

comprehension.subject: Frogs
comprehension.text: Frogs are small animals that can live both in water and on land. They begin life as tiny eggs in ponds or lakes. The eggs hatch into tadpoles, which have tails and live underwater. As tadpoles grow, they develop legs and lose their tails until they become frogs. Frogs use their long, sticky tongues to catch insects like flies and mosquitoes. Many frogs can jump very far because they have strong back legs. Some frogs are brightly coloured to warn predators to stay away.

questions.question: What do frog eggs hatch into?
What do frogs use to catch insects?
Why can frogs jump far?
Where can frogs live?
options: Both in water and on land
questions.correct: Both in water and on land

comprehension.subject: Mountains
comprehension.text: Mountains are very tall landforms that rise high above the ground around them. Some mountains are covered with snow all year because the air at the top is very cold. People climb mountains for adventure and to enjoy beautiful views. Many animals, such as goats and eagles, live in mountain areas. Rivers often begin in mountains when snow and ice melt into water. The tallest mountain in the world is Mount Everest, which is located in Asia.

questions.question: Why are some mountain tops covered in snow all year?
What often begins in mountains?
Why do people climb mountains?
What is the tallest mountain in the world?

comprehension.subject: Robots
comprehension.text: Robots are machines that can perform tasks automatically. Some robots work in factories building cars, while others help doctors in hospitals. There are even robots that explore space and the deep ocean where humans cannot easily go. Robots follow instructions from computer programs. Some robots can move, talk, or recognise objects using cameras and sensors. Although robots can do many useful jobs, they still need humans to design, build, and control them.

questions.question: Where do some robots work?
What do robots follow?
Why do robots explore space or the deep ocean?
Who designs and controls robots?

comprehension.subject: Oceans
comprehension.text: Oceans cover most of the Earth's surface and contain salty water. There are five main oceans on Earth. Oceans are home to many kinds of animals, including whales, fish, crabs, and sea turtles. Tiny plants called plankton float in the ocean and help produce much of the oxygen we breathe. Waves are created when wind blows across the water. Oceans are very important because they help control Earth's climate and provide food for millions of people.

questions.question: What kind of water is found in oceans?
What creates waves in the ocean?
What tiny plants help produce oxygen?
Why are oceans important?
options: They help control climate and provide food
questions.correct: They help control climate and provide food

comprehension.subject: The Human Body
comprehension.text: The human body is made up of many different parts that work together. The brain controls thinking, memory, and movement. The heart pumps blood around the body to carry oxygen and nutrients. Lungs help us breathe by taking in oxygen from the air. Bones give the body shape and protect important organs. Muscles help us move, run, jump, and lift things. Eating healthy food, exercising, and sleeping well help keep the body strong and healthy.

questions.question: What does the heart do?
What helps us breathe?
What gives the body shape?
What helps keep the body healthy?

comprehension.subject: Rainforests
comprehension.text: Rainforests are warm, wet forests that receive a lot of rain every year. They are home to many plants and animals, including monkeys, parrots, frogs, and jaguars. Trees in rainforests grow very tall and form a thick roof called a canopy. The canopy blocks much of the sunlight from reaching the ground below. Rainforests help clean the air by absorbing carbon dioxide and producing oxygen. Many medicines used by doctors today come from rainforest plants.

questions.question: What kind of weather do rainforests have?
What is the thick roof of trees called?
Why are rainforests important?
options: They absorb carbon dioxide and produce oxygen
questions.correct: They absorb carbon dioxide and produce oxygen

questions.question: What can come from rainforest plants?

comprehension.subject: Camels
comprehension.text: Camels are animals that are well adapted to living in hot deserts. They can travel long distances without drinking water. A camel stores fat in its hump, which gives it energy when food is hard to find. Camels also have long eyelashes and nostrils that can close to keep out blowing sand. Their wide feet help them walk easily on soft desert sand. People in desert areas often use camels to carry goods and transport people.

questions.question: Where do camels usually live?
What does a camel store in its hump?
Why do camels have long eyelashes?
What helps camels walk on sand?

comprehension.subject: Electricity
comprehension.text: Electricity is a form of energy that powers many things we use every day. Lights, televisions, computers, and refrigerators all need electricity to work. Electricity can come from power stations that use wind, water, sunlight, or fuel to create energy. Wires carry electricity safely into homes and schools. It is important to be careful around electricity because it can be dangerous. People should never touch broken wires or use electrical items with wet hands.

questions.question: What does electricity power?
What carries electricity into homes?
Why should people be careful around electricity?
Which of these can help create electricity?

comprehension.subject: Sharks
comprehension.text: Sharks are fish that live in oceans around the world. They have existed for millions of years, even before dinosaurs disappeared. Sharks have strong bodies and sharp teeth that help them catch food. Some sharks are very large, like the whale shark, while others are quite small. Sharks can smell tiny amounts of blood in the water from far away. Most sharks are not dangerous to humans and usually avoid people. Sharks are important because they help keep ocean ecosystems balanced.

questions.question: Where do sharks live?
What helps sharks catch food?
Are most sharks dangerous to humans?
Why are sharks important?

comprehension.subject: Bullying
comprehension.text: Bullying happens when someone keeps being mean or hurting another person on purpose. It can be through words, actions, or by leaving someone out of games. Bullying can make children feel sad, scared, or lonely. If someone is being bullied, they should tell a trusted adult like a parent or teacher. Good friends treat each other with respect and kindness. Standing up for others and including everyone helps stop bullying.

questions.question: What is bullying?
How can bullying make someone feel?
Who should you tell if someone is being bullied?
What do good friends do?

comprehension.subject: Stranger Safety
comprehension.text: Stranger safety means knowing how to stay safe around people you do not know. Children should never go anywhere with a stranger or accept gifts, food, or rides from them without a trusted adult. If a stranger tries to talk to a child in a way that feels uncomfortable, the child should walk away and tell a parent or teacher. Staying close to trusted adults in public places helps keep children safe.

questions.question: What should you do if a stranger offers you a ride?
options: Say no and tell a trusted adult
questions.correct: Say no and tell a trusted adult

questions.question: What should children accept from strangers?
What should you do if you feel uncomfortable?
options: Walk away and tell an adult

questions.question: Who helps keep children safe?

comprehension.subject: Online Safety
comprehension.text: Online safety means using the internet in a careful and smart way. Children should never share personal information like their full name, address, school, or phone number online. They should only talk to people they know in real life and avoid strange messages. If something online feels wrong or scary, they should tell a trusted adult immediately. It is also important to balance screen time with outdoor play and rest.

questions.question: What should children NOT share online?
Who should children talk to online?
options: People they know in real life
questions.correct: People they know in real life

questions.question: What should you do if something online feels wrong?
Why is outdoor play important?

comprehension.subject: Lying and Honesty
comprehension.text: Honesty means telling the truth even when it is difficult. Lying is when someone says something that is not true on purpose. Lies can cause problems and make people lose trust in each other. When someone tells the truth, even after making a mistake, it helps fix problems and build trust again. Being honest is an important value that helps friendships and families stay strong.

questions.question: What is honesty?
What is lying?
options: Saying something not true on purpose
questions.correct: Saying something not true on purpose

questions.question: What can lies do?
What helps fix mistakes?

comprehension.subject: Stealing and Respect
comprehension.text: Stealing means taking something that belongs to someone else without permission. It is unfair and can hurt other people’s feelings. Respect means caring about other people’s things and asking before using them. If someone wants to borrow something, they should always ask first and return it safely. Being respectful helps build trust and good friendships.

What should you do before borrowing something?
What does respect mean?
What does stealing do to others?

comprehension.subject: Peer Pressure
comprehension.text: Peer pressure happens when friends or other children try to make someone do something. Sometimes it can be good, like encouraging healthy habits. But sometimes it can be bad, like asking someone to break rules. It is important to make the right choices even if others are doing something wrong. Saying 'no' politely and walking away is a strong and brave choice.

questions.question: What is peer pressure?
Is peer pressure always bad?
What should you do if friends do something wrong?
What is a brave choice?

comprehension.subject: Littering
comprehension.text: Littering means throwing trash on the ground instead of putting it in a bin. It makes places dirty and can harm animals and nature. Clean parks and streets are important for everyone to enjoy. People should always throw rubbish in bins and recycle when possible. Keeping the environment clean shows respect for nature and other people.

questions.question: What is littering?
What can littering harm?
Where should rubbish go?
What does a clean environment show?

comprehension.subject: Cheating
comprehension.text: Cheating means breaking the rules to get an unfair advantage. It can happen in tests, games, or competitions. Cheating is not honest and can hurt others who are trying their best. It is better to try hard and do your own work, even if it is difficult. Learning from mistakes helps children grow stronger and smarter.

questions.question: What is cheating?
Where can cheating happen?
What does cheating do to others?
What is better than cheating?

comprehension.subject: Kindness and Sharing
comprehension.text: Kindness means being helpful, caring, and thoughtful toward others. Sharing means giving part of what you have to someone else. Kind people make others feel happy and included. Sharing toys, food, or time can make friendships stronger. Even small kind actions, like saying nice words or helping someone, can make a big difference.

questions.question: What is kindness?
What does sharing mean?
options: Giving part of what you have
questions.correct: Giving part of what you have

questions.question: How do kind actions affect others?
What can strengthen friendships?
